Claims to offer protection when players aren’t carrying silvered weapons.
His demeanor turns serious when players show signs of preparation with silvered arms.
Introduces the story of a supernatural female killer in the woods.
foreshadowing: May appear again, particularly after revealing werewolf threats; may serve as foreshadowing of future wolves.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: One of two werewolves in disguise.
Sullen and quiet compared to Zsolt.
Silent but watchful during their interaction with players near Lake Baratok.

Appears when Zsolt decides on attacking players.
foreshadowing: May be important for more complex encounters involving werewolves throughout the campaign.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: Van Richten's horse.

Used by him to help transport Arabelle during missions.
Kept safe in the tower after the initial abduction events.
Part of Van Richten’s plan to evade detection while traveling.
foreshadowing: Key travel asset; could be referenced again during escape sequences or transportation.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: River that flows through Barovia and borders Vallaki.

Nearby areas like Luna River Crossroads are frequently used locations by characters.
Important for navigation paths and setting for various events.
foreshadowing: Important waterway that will feature further use in future encounters or storylines involving movement around the valley

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md

Players encounter werewolves here as enemies while coming back.
Location where many players first discover clues about Rictavio and Ezmerelda.

Used as the starting point for journeys to different points of the map.
foreshadowing: Will connect to upcoming locations and serve as transition zone between parts of campaign

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: A path leading from Lake Baratok to Vallaki, used by players traveling back.

Provides cover and setting for stealthy or hidden activities.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md

Connected to themes of identity, unity, and community within campaign.
Places where players may interact or encounter Vistani related to Arabelle's rescue.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: Area where players may seek information or assistance.
Known to be dangerous and unknown territory in Barovia.
Connected to the wider themes of strange and mysterious locations.
Described briefly but plays role in encouraging explorations of wilderness boundaries.
Part of the setting for the campaign's atmospheric storytelling.
foreshadowing: May lead players to additional lore areas or hidden items related to the campaign’s setting

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc E - The Missing Vistana.md
bullets: Inn in Vallaki where players can gather information about Rictavio.
Used as location to find clues about Rictavio’s activities and whereabouts.
Players learn key details from patrons like Danika Dorakova here.
Settlement landmark for communication between characters and narrative.
Potential meeting place or reference point in story progression.
foreshadowing: Could reappear throughout campaign or used as transition spots

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Lady Fiona Wachter is a widow of Nikolai Wachter I and mother of Nikolai Wachter II, Karl Wachter, and Stella Wachter.
She serves as the burgomaster of Vallaki after taking power from Baron Vargas Vallakovich.
She has a complex relationship with Strahd von Zarovich but maintains pragmatic loyalty.
Her family history includes tragedy—her parents and elder brother died in a mysterious accident during her youth.
She was trained by the swamp witch Baba Lysaga (referred to as 'Lysa') and follows the worship of Ezra, goddess of the Mists.
foreshadowing: Her connection to Baba Lysaga foreshadows future events involving Lysaga's witches-in-training in Castle Ravenloft.
The narrative links her to Leo Dilisnya through family heritage, which will be explored further in Arc H - The Lost Soul.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Urwin Martikov is the innkeeper of the Blue Water Inn in Vallaki.
He delivers letters to players if certain conditions are met related to their actions against Baron Vargas Vallakovich or interactions with refugees.
He can provide information about Lady Fiona Wachter and shares legend about her family's tragic past.
He recognizes and describes Ernst Larnak as the courier who delivered the invitation to Lady Wachter's home.
foreshadowing: Urwin's role introduces key background elements and future interaction points with other characters.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Izek Strazni is the right-hand man and personal enforcer of Baron Vargas Vallakovich.
He lost an arm and sister to a wolf attack during childhood, which led to his early adoption by Vargas.
His physical transformation included regaining a demonic limb that allowed him to conjure fire.
He holds significant power within Vallaki as Captain of the Guard; his murder is requested by Lady Wachter.
Izek serves as a major antagonist in the mission arc, eventually transformed into a more formidable enemy.
foreshadowing: His history with Leo Dilisnya connects him to a darker narrative thread involving family bloodlines.
His role in killing Stella links back to Arc H - The Lost Soul where her fate is resolved.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
wikilink_seeds: [[Arc H - The Lost Soul#H5. Wachterhaus|Arc H - The Lost Soul]]
bullets: Wachterhaus is the estate of Lady Fiona Wachter located north of Vallaki along the northern road.
It has undergone changes, including the removal of Nikolai Wachter's corpse and adjustments to room layouts.
Important rooms include Stella’s Bedroom, Storage Room, Master Bedroom, and various hallways.
The house has a distinctive façade with thick gardens, herbs and vines, slouching roof, moss-covered walls, and red-painted door.
foreshadowing: Wachterhaus contains significant items including the religious texts *Voices of the Mist* and *The Grimoire of Night's Whispers*, which connect with themes in Arc H - The Lost Soul.
Its layout reveals details about Lady Wachter’s past, particularly regarding her mentor Baba Lysaga.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
source_anchor: F3. Return to the Blue Water Inn
bullets: Ernst Larnak is Lady Fiona Wachter’s spy, courier, and confidant.
He delivers invitations and updates on Izek’s schedule to the players.
His role expands beyond mere information sharing—he aids in planning actions against Izek.
Ernst is revealed as an antagonist in the later Arc S - The Moonlit Murders following the events of this arc.
foreshadowing: Ernst's knowledge suggests his future role as a double agent in a later arc.
His familiarity with the town and access to information indicate deep integration into Vallaki’s politics.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Stella Wachter is Lady Fiona Wachter’s youngest child and a victim of a mysterious illness.
She suffers in a vegetative state since her illness developed two months ago after meeting Victor Vallakovich.
Stella's condition is directly linked to events involving Izek Strazni according to Lady Wachter.
Her illness involves soul loss, which will be explored more thoroughly in Arc H - The Lost Soul.
foreshadowing: Stella’s plight sets up the emotional stakes for the arc and foreshadows her rescue in Arc H - The Lost Soul.
The mystery of her condition is central to Lady Wachter's character development throughout subsequent arcs.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
source_anchor: F3. Return to the Blue Water Inn
bullets: Danika Dorakova is a character at the Blue Water Inn who informs players about Ernst Larnak's presence.
She recognizes Ernst as the cloaked figure from the previous evening who delivered the invitation.
Her role supports linking the two events and helps transition into the main mission.
foreshadowing: Danika introduces other important elements of Vallaki society, including awareness of the town’s politics and individuals such as Ernst Larnak.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Nikolai Wachter is Lady Fiona Wachter's eldest son who meets players at the estate.
He seems to be the one who brought Stella into the parlor before she wandered off.
His recognition of players and personal history with the family contributes to background details.
He was named after Lady Fiona Wachter’s late husband.
foreshadowing: Nikolai’s character links directly to the family's history, including Lady Wachter's past with her mentor Baba Lysaga.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Karl Wachter is Lady Fiona Wachter’s second son, mischievous and somewhat of a troublemaker.
He plays an important role in understanding his mother's concerns and her view of the family dynamics.
The fact that he was seen with Ireena Kolyana indicates a potential connection or shared interest.
foreshadowing: Karl’s presence sets up possible future story lines involving his involvement and interaction with other major characters in the campaign.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Amber Shards are relics found inside a pouch recovered from Izek Strazni after his assassination.
These items are tied to specific individuals and locations, with one belonging to the Hell Hound Whisperer Seriach.
They have significance in the game's lore related to powerful entities and their influence on Barovia.
Accessing amber shards will likely lead to deeper narrative exploration in other arcs of the campaign.
foreshadowing: The mention of amber shards introduces the idea of potent magical artifacts that may play a role in future arcs like Arc D - The Tome of Strahd or Arc S - A Sword of Sunlight.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc F - Lady Wachter's Wish.md
bullets: Vallaki is the primary setting of this arc, characterized by its dark history and political unrest.
It serves as a hub where Lady Wachter's scheme unfolds, and is home to several important characters and locations.
The town is governed under the rule of Baron Vargas Vallakovich until Lady Fiona Wachter takes over.
Key institutions include the Blue Water Inn, Wachterhaus, and various gates connecting to the surrounding environment.
foreshadowing: Vallaki acts as a backdrop for future developments including the events of Arc I - The Walls of Krezk and Arc Q - A Shining Beacon.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: A companion of the player characters, sought by Izek Strazni.
She is revealed to be the sister of Izek Strazni, though she does not know this initially.
She is kidnapped by Izek and held in his room at the Baron's mansion.
She can wield a dagger and fights alongside the players after being freed.
She chooses to journey with the players following her rescue from Izek.
foreshadowing: Her relationship with Izek is a major plot point, revealed during the rescue arc.
Her decision to accompany the players lays groundwork for Act III.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: A ruthless enforcer of the Baron, obsessed with finding Ireena.
He kidnaps Ireena and holds her captive in the Baron's mansion.
He possesses fire-based abilities including hurl flame and various fire attacks.
Has a dual form where his physical capabilities and damage types change after reaching 0 HP.
Uses a silvered battleaxe as his weapon of choice.
foreshadowing: His obsession with Ireena drives the major conflict of this arc.
His fire-based abilities foreshadow the burning of the wicker sun at the Festival of the Blazing Sun.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: Owner of the Blue Water Inn, loyal to the player characters.
He and his wife Danika protect Ireena from Izek when he visits the inn.
Provides information about Izek's schedule and activities to the players.
Offers to assist the players in tracking Izek by uncovering details about his movements.
Is connected with the Keepers of the Feather, revealed later.
foreshadowing: His knowledge of Izek and ability to provide intel points to Lady Wachter's connection to him.
His relationship with the Keepers will be further developed in Arc J.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: Co-owner of the Blue Water Inn with Urwin, loyal to the player characters.
Works alongside Urwin to shelter Ireena from Izek’s search.
Helps in securing Ireena during Izek's attack on the inn.
Assists in the rescue by working with wereravens to save Ireena from the inn.
Can access information about Izek through her network.
foreshadowing: Part of the group protecting Ireena from harm, indicating connections beyond normal townsfolk.
Her involvement suggests the existence of a hidden network or alliance in Vallaki.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: A location in Vallaki where Ireena was staying and where Izek searched for her.
Is damaged by Izek during his visit, with property damage including broken furniture and a burned chest.
Contains secret stairways and rooms used to hide Ireena from Izek.
Inhabited by Urwin and Danika and serves as a safe zone for the players.
Burned down by Izek after he discovers where Ireena is hidden.
foreshadowing: Its destruction sets up conflict with the Baron’s household and ties into potential future events involving fire magic and burning.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: The seat of power in Vallaki, where Baron Vallakovich resides.
Where Izek keeps Ireena after kidnapping her.
Contains a secret staircase where Ireena might be hidden during Izek’s attacks.
Home to various servants like Victor, Tereska, Clavdia, and others.
Izek's bedroom is located here, from which he fights the players during rescue attempts.
foreshadowing: The mansion is central to both the kidnapping and rescue arcs of the story.
Its fire-based damage during combat foreshadows the Festival of the Blazing Sun events.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: A political figure and opponent of the Baron, known for her opposition to Strazni.
Invites the players to tea in an attempt to gain their trust and aid.
Offers to assist the players in removing Izek from power, potentially taking the position of Burgomaster.
Is revealed to have an interest in protecting the residents of Vallaki.
Provides alternate avenues of assistance beyond direct confrontation with Izek.
foreshadowing: Her involvement in Arc F sets up further plot developments and political maneuvering.
She is shown as a potential ally, contrasting with the corrupt Baron.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: A priest of St. Andral’s Church in Vallaki.
Is interrupted by Izek during a sermon, who proceeds to search his office for Ireena.
Has a hidden room in the church used as a third bedroom for Ireena.
Protects Ireena while she's at the church during Izek’s first hunt.
Has been instrumental in keeping Ireena safe prior to her kidnapping.
foreshadowing: His connection to Ireena’s concealment within the church plays a role in the political tension of Vallaki.
He may provide insight into the greater conflict and the nature of Strahd's influence.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc G - The Strazni Siblings.md
bullets: Son of the Baron, found in his workroom at the mansion.
Can be a resource for information or may act as an obstacle if discovered by the players.
Has access to spells like Cone of Cold and is a potentially dangerous opponent if cornered.
Is involved in the mansion's defense and knows about the secret passages.
Offers to help in fighting against Ireena’s captor.
foreshadowing: His role in the mansion gives an understanding of Baron Vallakovich’s inner circle.
He may later play a greater part in the political upheaval in Vallaki.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A young woman whose soul was severed from her body by a gallows speaker during a failed teleportation experiment.
Trapped in the Ethereal Plane and seeking to be reunited with her body.
The daughter of Lady Fiona Wachter and close friend of Victor Vallakovich.
Has been able to communicate with Victor and Erasmus through a spirit mirror since her soul was separated.
May be fading from the Ethereal Plane if not returned to her body soon.
A sixteen-year-old specter who was previously captured in the Ethereal Plane by Leo Dilisnya.
Floats down to players after the etherealness ritual and is shown to be translucent with fading legs.
Has lost her tangibility and slowly fades into the Deep Ethereal, requiring a restoration ritual.
Is Lady Wachter's daughter, best friend of Victor Vallakovich, and a friend of Erasmus van Richten.
Reveals her desire for Victor to master his magic and help her family escape Barovia.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A teenage son of the Baron of Vallaki who has been working with Erasmus and Stella to restore her soul.
Possesses a spellbook belonging to the lich Khazan, which he used to research teleportation magic.
Created a teleportation circle with Stella that inadvertently caused her soul to be exposed to the Ethereal Plane.
Is determined to help Stella return to her body and has been studying rituals and methods for entering the Ethereal Plane.
Has become desperate as time runs short, fearing Stella's soul may fully dissipate soon.
Player ally who offers assistance in the raid on Old Bonegrinder via teleportation (misty step, fly) and invisibility spells.

Equipped with advanced spells, notably *greater invisibility*, *counterspell* (at 3rd or 4th level, and potentially 5th under updated rules), *magic missile*, *remove curse* (not used in this guide), and specific preparation for combat as outlined for the 2014 version.
Warns against early spending of 5th-level spell slots before battle with hags because of their potential ability to dispel enchantments.
Maintains a critical role during the binding circle ritual, staying close to the windmill as its guardian due to protection needs.
Has a spellbook which differs in content from standard versions, including *sending* and *fear* spells with an absence of *remove curse*.
A mage who helps players perform rituals to restore Stella Wachter's soul.
Has a copy of the spellbook 'Ethereal Entities' and uses it during rituals.
Believes he can repurpose an existing pentagram for the etherealness ritual if Lady Wachter is not present.
Uses his *mage hand* spell to hold open 'Ethereal Entities' during the etherealness ritual.
Has a strong connection with Stella Wachter and appears jealous of Erasmus's presence.
foreshadowing: His role extends beyond the immediate scenario into possible alliances or future conflicts with other factions involved in Castle Ravenloft's mysteries.
His expertise and magical tools make him a vital asset for the party’s further progress within Ravenloft.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The mother of Stella Wachter and widow of the late Balthazar Wachter.
Inherited control over Wachterhaus after her family died mysteriously.
Established a protective circle around Wachterhaus to prevent ethereal creatures from entering, particularly the gallows speaker Leo Dilisnya.
Initially skeptical of Victor's claims about Stella’s soul condition but can be convinced to help if properly persuaded or by previous actions like killing Izek Strazni.
Serves as a powerful ally if approached correctly and aids in creating binding rituals to trap night hags.
A cult leader assisting players in combating or escaping the windmill, capable of calling upon four cult fanatics to raise a binding circle for combat.
Possesses spellcasting ability including raising binding circles with arcane power and providing magical assistance such as *mage armor* and sending rituals.
Works in conjunction with Victor Vallakovich to help the players during the Battle with the Hags in order to safely obtain Morgantha's contract.
Can provide a charm of mass heroism upon completing certain conditions, but is also known for employing deception or negotiation strategies.
Reveals three secrets when pressured by the party, related to Strahd's escape plan and secret shrines.
Owner of Wachterhaus, which has a leyline beneath it that can be used for rituals.
Disables the protective circle around Wachterhaus to let creatures pass through the Ethereal Plane.
Sets cult fanatics to guard the cellar entrances during the ritual.
Informs players about the room's carvings and history, including knowledge of Leo Dilisnya.
Has been unable to remove carvings on the walls but confirms they're harmless.
foreshadowing: Lady Wachter is connected strongly to the larger story involving her daughter Stella and the wider conflict around Castle Ravenloft.
She serves as a bridge between the local folk of Vallaki and the overarching supernatural threats of the Mists.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A ghost who communicates with players by inscribing messages in frost.
Helped rescue Stella from the Ethereal Plane after her soul was severed by a gallows speaker.
Wants Victor and the players to assist in restoring Stella’s soul.
Is familiar with gallows speakers and has shared knowledge about them with Victor.
Has been actively working on helping keep Stella safe while she exists only in the Ethereal Plane.
A ghost appearing in the Ethereal Plane who helps Stella navigate and avoid being taken by wayward spirits.
Has a spectral form that appears as a youthful man with a warm light and elven features.
Reveals he's been helping Stella since shortly after she arrived in the Ethereal Plane.
Informs players about the unusual activities of lost spirits in the Ethereal Plane.
Gives players insight on how to move in the Ethereal Plane.
foreshadowing: Foreshadows his father's identity and role in future arcs like Arc T - The Three Fanes
His presence hints at the Van Richten family tragedy explored in Arc E - The Missing Vistana

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A peddler who sells dream pastries to refugees and has been involved in the abduction of children.
The mother of a coven of night hags, including Bella Sunbane and Offalia Wormwiggle.
Used nightmares and heartstones to kidnap and enslave children from the refugee camp.
Has a lair located inside a windmill in Old Bonegrinder, where she keeps her heartstone and possibly her young daughters.
Lends her heartstone under condition that players deliver a soul leech to Rahadin, Strahd's chamberlain.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A widower refugee suffering from a wasting disease caused by nightmares.
Sold his two children Myrtle and Fyodor to Morgantha for dream pastries.
He is now deeply distressed, believing his children are suffering in the hands of Morgantha.
His story leads players to discover that Morgantha's lair is a windmill used to entrap children.
Has a night hag's nightmare haunting that paralyzes him and slowly wastes his body.
Barovian refugee whose children Fyodor and Myrtle are held captive in the windmill by Morgantha.
His capture is tied to a larger story about refugees within Strahd's domain.
Morgantha uses his plight as a bargaining tool to manipulate the players, even suggesting that his presence might lead to punishment if they do not comply with her requests.
He plays no direct active role in the narrative unless the parties choose to interact with the coven further post-rescue of his children.
Known by the hags through their relationship with him and his family.
foreshadowing: Franz represents one of the many innocent victims caught within Strahd's schemes, indicating the scope of his influence on refugees beyond the immediate party.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Young children abducted by Morgantha, who are imprisoned in the windmill near Old Bonegrinder.
Their father Franz believes they are suffering under the hags' manipulation and cruelty.
Are likely held as victims inside the windmill where their mothers dwell and practice dark rituals.
May become important assets in bargaining or rescuing if freed from Morgantha’s grip.
Their fate is central to uncovering how to properly bind the night hag coven in Morgantha's lair.
Child imprisoned with Fyodor in the windmill, used to obtain the contract and to bargain for safe passage or rescue.
Together with her brother, Myrtle can be used as a lever against Morgantha if players attempt to negotiate after the binding circle is raised.
Held captive due to their father Franz’s role within the narrative of refugees affected by Strahd's actions.
They’re not particularly involved in the greater story beyond their roles as leverage points for negotiation.
If they are rescued, they return to their father, Franz, who is part of the local refugee community.
foreshadowing: Their presence in the windmill ties directly into broader campaign elements concerning refugees and Strahd's influence over life in Vallaki.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Daughters of Morgantha, likely part of her coven of night hags.
Possess dark magical abilities and may have played roles in abducting children including Myrtle and Fyodor.
Must be located in the windmill at Old Bonegrinder along with other members to ensure their names are known for binding rituals.
Part of a group that can threaten Lady Wachter's allies during an assault on Morgantha’s lair.
Their fate is tied with freeing and defeating Morgantha through a full confrontation.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Sources of information about Victor, including confirming he is the Baron’s son.
Provide background on characters and events relevant to current storylines.
Are part of the local community of Vallaki where they serve roles in public life and may offer hints or support.
Offer insight regarding the inner dynamics among different factions.
Not directly involved in the main conflict but provide context for important NPCs like Victor and Lady Wachter.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: An enforcer in Vallaki who was tasked with sending the refugees away from the town.
Has been involved in conflict between the Wachter family and Baron Vallakovich.
His actions led to the death of Lady Wachter’s parents by placing them under suspicion before their mysterious demise.
Can be killed to gain favor with Lady Wachter or to fulfill requirements for gaining her help in binding the night hags.
An antagonist relevant to Lady Wachter's past, and serves a critical role in how Victor and others view her.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A location where a night hag's lair is rumored to be nestled inside a windmill near the Svalich Road.
Contains hidden chambers including a compartment in the millstone believed by Victor to be where Morgantha keeps secrets such as heartstones or her young daughters.
The setting for an upcoming assault planned by Victor and Lady Wachter against Morgantha's coven.
Hostile territory for both players and cultists due to its proximity to Morgantha’s lair and active presence of hags.
Key location in the campaign as a site where children were taken and dark rituals are performed.
A windmill located on a hill in the Svalich Wood, serving as the lair of the three hags Morgantha Stormreaver, Belladonna Sunbane, and Offalia Wormwiggle.
Contains a stone compartment in its millstone that holds the hags' contract and a *charm of mass heroism*.
The windmill's hill is surrounded by a binding circle during battles with the hags, which prevents the hags from using their *etherealness* ability.
The area around the windmill is known for its proximity to the Mountain Fane, marked by megaliths shrouded in fog.
The third floor of the mill holds imprisoned children, Fyodor and Myrtle, guarded by the hags.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The ancestral estate of the Wachter family in Vallaki, now owned by Lady Fiona Wachter.
Protected by a magical circle erected centuries ago to keep out ethereal creatures like Leo Dilisnya's gallows speaker spirit.
An important hub where binding rituals involving night hags can occur after Stella is located or rescued.
Contains a complex history related to past murders and family trauma rooted in historical conflicts with Strahd.
A significant site for Lady Wachter’s involvement in helping Victor restore Stella.
The estate owned by Lady Wachter and the location of a leyline beneath.
Has a cellar that serves as a cult headquarters for rituals.
Contains protective circles around it to control passage into and out of the Ethereal Plane.
Built on top of a leyline which is used in performing the etherealness ritual.
The room underneath the cellar contains carved carvings including 'In Silence, Death' and images of Leo Dilisnya.
foreshadowing: Serves as a setting for both the restoration ritual and the final battle against Leo Dilisnya
Foreshadows Lady Wachter's betrayal history and her family's relationship with the Van Richten family

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A metaphysical realm where Stella's soul remains trapped after being severed.
Interacts with the Material Plane, enabling spiritual communication through mirrors.
Contains dangers like wandering entities and hostile spirits that make it difficult for Stella to return.
Required to access in order to perform rituals or rescue Stella from her current condition.
Where night hags are active, particularly those linked to Morgantha.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A creature found in the Ethereal Plane and involved in stealing souls through nightmares.
Known for having heartstones that can be used to enter or traverse the Ethereal Plane.
Part of a coven including Morgantha, her daughters Bella Sunbane and Offalia Wormwiggle.
Can use heartstones to travel ethereally instantly.
Are dangerous opponents with magical abilities; binding them requires understanding their names and contracts.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The vengeful spirit of Leo Dilisnya, a traitor killed by Strahd in Castle Ravenloft.
Manifests as a malevolent presence haunting Wachterhaus and attacking anyone within range.
Connected to both the historical events behind the Wachter family's curse and Lady Wachter's decision to protect herself with a binding circle.
Its haunting was the source of the deaths of Balthazar, Lavinia, and Frederich Wachter.
An enemy of the Wachters who seeks revenge even after Leo’s original death.
Leo Dilisnya's transformed state after death, formed by the spirits of castle guards.
Has a spectral appearance with skeletal hands and tattered robe showing faces in its cloak.
Aims to kill Stella Wachter and Lady Wachter's sons as an act of vengeance for his family.
Clutches the Tome of Strahd which he stole after murdering Lady Wachter's parents.
Has two distinct forms: Speaker of the Gallows (first form) and Ba'al Verzi Avenger (second form).
foreshadowing: Signifies an increased threat level for Lady Wachter's family in the campaign narrative
Represents a major antagonist whose backstory links directly to characters in Arc E and beyond

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A book written by Mordenkainen describing creatures within the Ethereal Plane and their behaviors.
Contains information on specific creatures including night hags and rituals that use heartstones to access the Ethereal Plane.
Used as a key resource that helps Victor and players understand methods for entering ethereal realms.
Influential in developing the ritual needed to perform an etherealness spell or bind hags.
Described as containing bestiaries divided into categories like Etherborn, Phantomfolk, and Veil-Walkers.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A magical mirror capable of reflecting visions from the Ethereal Plane to the Material Plane.
Used by Victor and Stella to communicate during Stella’s absence due to soul separation.
Allows Victor to visually present Stella to players using only gesture and lipreading techniques.
Crucial tool for keeping Stella's presence known while she remains trapped beyond sight.
Plays a key role in the early stages of helping Stella return to her body.
A wondrous item found in Victor Vallakovich's workroom that reflects locations in Ethereal Plane.
Can be used daily once per day for either shrinking or growing in size.
Given to the players as a reward for restoring Stella Wachter’s soul.
Originally obtained through magical means involving Erasmus and possibly Dr. Rudolph van Richten.
Has relevance in other story arcs, notably in lifting Van Richten's curse.
foreshadowing: Connected to future events around Van Richten's curse, as detailed in Arc T
Revealed as a tool for viewing Ethereal Plane locations, which could be useful later in campaign

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A magical construct developed by Victor and Stella in preparation for escape from Strahd’s realm.
Failed during a test involving human sacrifice, resulting in Stella losing her soul to the Ethereal Plane.
The focus of Victor’s research as he attempts to restore Stella through alternate means.
Revealed to be the original cause of Stella’s soul being severed and serves as motivation for further action.
Could potentially serve as a base mechanism if reworked or corrected with new knowledge gained from other magical sources.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Magical artifact used by night hags to escape or enter the Ethereal Plane.
Required component for using a specific spell that mimics etherealness.
Can be found in the possession of Morgantha and forms part of her coven’s ritual.
Used under duress by players when negotiating with Morgantha, resulting in them taking magical dreams as collateral.
Described in detail in the book *Ethereal Entities* under the section about night hags.
An artifact allowing the wielder to borrow and use spells of a limited number of levels, with restrictions like returning it within three days or suffering permanent dream loss if betrayed.
Morgantha lends heartstones to players for short periods during negotiations, providing them access to magical power tied closely to her domain.
Heartstones are described as items that can be used in conjuring and casting powerful magical spells beyond a typical character’s capability.
Must be returned within three days, otherwise dream loss occurs; also serves as collateral when players engage in risky deals with Morgantha.
Used for gaining access to spells during the conflict with the hags but holds significant narrative weight in relation to Strahd's plans.
A necessary component in the etherealness and restoration rituals to bring Stella back.
Can be used to perform the *etherealness* ritual when placed at the center of a pentagram.
Appears as an obsidian object with a shimmering, spectral luminescence when activated.
The heartstone causes physical disturbances such as ripples in the floor and mist roiling around.
Essential for moving players into the Ethereal Plane where Stella's soul resides.
foreshadowing: Heartstones are central in the lore of Ravenloft and indicate an underappreciated source of arcane power available through negotiation or coercion.
They could play into future scenarios involving magical conflicts, especially considering how Morgantha uses them as leverage in her dealings.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: An item that Morgantha requested players deliver to Strahd’s chamberlain Rahadin.
Used as a form of bargaining after acquiring heartstone from the players.
Represents an important point in the narrative for fulfilling Morgantha’s conditions and avoiding consequences.
Linked to larger plans involving Strahd's court and possibly hidden dark magics.
A key element used later in story progression if players choose to take on Morgantha’s task.
A Tiny fiend resembling a leech that Morgantha imprisoned in a glass vial, used as part of her bargaining scheme with players seeking to borrow the heartstone.
Players are instructed to deliver it to Rahadin’s office at Castle Ravenloft and uncork it, allowing the leech to attach itself to his desk so that it can entrap his soul upon his death.
The leech is intended to transform into a soul larva, which Morgantha hopes to use for nefarious purposes after Rahadin dies.
Its appearance reflects its role as a tool of manipulation within Strahd’s dark machinations.
Players must complete the task in fourteen days or risk having their dreams devoured.
foreshadowing: The soul leech is instrumental to Morgantha's plan involving Rahadin, pointing toward larger consequences tied to Strahd's own escape strategies and possible betrayal.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Majesto, Lady Wachter's familiar and shapechanged into crow form.
Follows Lady Wachter in combat and travels with the group.
Helps defend against druids or other threats encountered along the journey.
Represents part of Lady Wachter’s resources available during adventures.
Has a presence that helps identify her as an important figure among local factions.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Supporters and allies of Lady Wachter who help in magical binding rituals.
Named members include Andrej, Boris, Miruna, and Ruxandra.
Provide combat assistance and knowledge in creating protective barriers.
Are crucial figures in the final confrontation with night hags at Old Bonegrinder.
Helped in organizing resources necessary for the assault on the windmill.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Strahd’s chamberlain, who has been targeted by Morgantha to receive a soul leech as part of a deal.
Connected to the political structure beneath Strahd’s castle and represents potential leverage or threat in dealings.
Has an interest in dark magic that could influence future story progression involving Strahd's court.
Key figure when negotiating with Morgantha for heartstone access or other items.
Placed under pressure by Morgantha to collect souls, making his involvement critical to later parts of the campaign.
Strahd’s chamberlain whose office is located in the cellar of Castle Ravenloft near the kitchens.
The destination for the soul leech which Morgantha asks players to deliver as part of her deal.
His role in the narrative involves being manipulated by Morgantha, with his potential death and entrapment of his soul contributing to dark plot elements involving the soul bag.
Is a key part of the lore connecting Strahd’s domain to the larger conflict concerning soul manipulation, death traps, and forbidden knowledge.
His interaction with players and their use of the soul leech leads into potential ramifications related to future story development.
foreshadowing: Rahadin's role points toward larger thematic concerns of loyalty, betrayal, and consequences of Strahd’s machinations within his castle walls.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The primary leader of the Bonegrinder Coven, a night hag with the ability to assume multiple forms and possess magical powers including *etherealness*, *clairvoyance*, *dispel magic*, *lightning bolt*, and *eyebite*.
She leads the coven in both normal and Nightmare Three phases and has the ability to use her *soul bag* to trap souls upon death for nefarious purposes.
Known to have imprisoned the children Fyodor and Myrtle, and is willing to bargain with player characters for their release, especially after a binding circle is raised by Lady Wachter.
Holds a charm of mass heroism which bestows the effect of a *potion of heroism* on creatures within 30 feet upon activation.

Reveals three secrets when parleying with the players; these include Strahd's secret temple, his plan to escape from the Mists via three shrines, and that his eldest wife Sasha Ivliskova is a traitor.
foreshadowing: Morgantha’s role in the larger narrative is central, as she holds knowledge about Strahd's pact, his escape plot, and the traitor within his household.
She can be bribed with heartstone under certain conditions but has a tendency to demand harsh tasks or body parts as collateral for trust.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Member of the Bonegrinder Coven and a night hag who specializes in *blindness/deafness*, *phantasmal force*, *crown of madness*, and *putrid radiance*.
Attacks players with *Claws*, *Blindness/Deafness*, *Putrid Radiance*, or *Spectral Shadows*.
In the second phase as a crocodile, uses *Bite* and *Tail* attacks, along with *Tail Swipe*, *Hallucinatory Mist*, and *Tail Lash* reactions.
In Nightmare Three form, has a large crocodile body and uses her *Tail* and *Bite* attacks effectively in combat.
Is susceptible to a spell like *Counterspell* or *Absorb Elements* if attacked by magical means.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Member of the Bonegrinder Coven and a night hag who uses *Ray of Enfeeblement*, *Acid Arrow*, *Cause Fear*, *Bestow Curse*, and *Hold Person*.
Attacks players with *Claws*, *Ray of Enfeeblement*, *Acid Arrow*, or *Ray of Sickness* attacks during combat.
In the second phase as an elephant, uses a *Gore* attack along with *Stomp*, *Fiend’s Trumpet*, *Tusks*, and *Matron’s Rebuke* reactions.
Known to be easily distracted and can be led to other areas of the windmill using illusions or performance checks.
Has access to *Melf's Acid Arrow* as a spell during its normal coven form.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Child imprisoned within the windmill's third floor by Morgantha's coven along with Myrtle, and is held as leverage to obtain the contract.
Son of Franze, a Barovian refugee whose fate ties into the broader campaign lore regarding Strahd's manipulation.
Fyodor and his sister are not interested in the larger narrative but desire only to reunite with their father.
In an alternate version of events, Freek is noted as being named Fyodor in this text, indicating he is part of the same group of children.
The players may be tasked with rescuing them from the windmill or negotiating over their release.
foreshadowing: Fyodor and Myrtle serve as hostages whose potential fates are tied to key decisions made by the party, especially regarding Strahd's schemes.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Lady Wachter’s imp familiar who acts as a scout, able to utilize *crow*, *spider*, and *rat* forms while remaining invisible with the *invisibility* ability.
Used in combat to attack hags concentrating on spells with the *sting* action.
Can provide information to players about the guard duties of dretches outside the attic and the location of Lady Wachter's hidden group.
Has a somewhat selfish nature but can be reminded of the mission using persuasion checks if necessary.
Serves both as support during escape and combat scenarios.
foreshadowing: Majesto may be called upon again in other arcs of the campaign due to his connections with Lady Wachter and his familiarity with combat situations.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A trio of night hags: Morgantha Stormreaver, Belladonna Sunbane, and Offalia Wormwiggle who share a collective set of abilities and statistics that are more robust than individual hags.
Their shared hit points mean damage taken by one hag is subtracted from the coven's total.
They possess *Innate Spellcasting*, *Claws*, *Change Shape*, *Etherealness*, and *Nightmare Haunting* features, and do not benefit from shared spellcasting normally.

When reduced to 0 HP, the coven transitions into a second form called *The Nightmare Three*, where they assume terrifying new forms—Bella into a crocodile, Offalia into an elephant, and Morgantha into shadowy enshrouding form.
Coven members are particularly dangerous in their lair actions: Warding Wind, Gust of Wind, Amphibious Cacophony (to lure targets), and Dispel Magic (once per day).
foreshadowing: The coven forms an integral part of the larger threat landscape in Curse of Strahd, particularly in regards to Morgantha’s plans involving souls, the soul bag, and the overall narrative arc concerning Strahd.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A golden medallion adorned with interlocking rings, capable of providing multiple creatures within 30 feet with the benefit of a *potion of heroism* as an action.
Found in the stone compartment inside the millstone of Old Bonegrinder after retrieving the iron key used to unlock it.
Can only be activated once and disappears from wielder’s person after usage.
Useful for enhancing group capabilities in combat or challenging scenarios requiring heightened bravery or strength.
Revealed when the players obtain the hags' contract, signifying it as a reward for overcoming the hags.
foreshadowing: Such an item reflects the theme of empowerment within Strahd’s domain—the magic that allows the bold to stand against dark powers, although here used for tactical gain rather than absolute good.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A leather book made from human skin, written in Abyssal script and signed with the true names of the three hags: Morgantha Stormreaver, Belladonna Sunbane, and Offalia Wormwiggle.
Located inside the millstone of Old Bonegrinder, unlocked and retrieved using an iron key or a successful Dexterity (Thieves' Tools) check.
Holds both the names of the hags necessary to defeat them permanently, and the treasure of the room as specified in Chapter 6: O3. Bedroom.
Can’t be destroyed by normal attacks, only damaged by the hags’ own claw attacks; destroying it dispels the coven into individual night hags.
Essential for completing the raid on Old Bonegrinder and obtaining key identifiers for defeating the hags once in battle.
foreshadowing: This item’s existence implies knowledge of magical contracts or binding arrangements within Strahd’s domain, which may be connected to larger arcane forces affecting the campaign.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Four followers supporting Lady Wachter in performing ritual binding circles.
They act as guardians of the circle, essential to protecting against magical counterattacks from Morgantha’s coven.
If any one is knocked unconscious, it will cause the entire ritual to fail and the hags to escape.
Are seen participating in the raising of the binding circle around Old Bonegrinder in cooperation with Lady Wachter.
Though not explicitly characterized further, they likely operate under orders aligned with Lady Wachter’s agenda and possibly part of a broader organization.
foreshadowing: The cult fanatics hint at a hidden underground resistance or opposition to Strahd in Vallaki, whose actions directly connect to the local conflict narrative.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The second form of the Bonegrinder Coven after it reaches 0 hit points, transforming into one medium and two huge fiends.
In this form, Bella becomes a Huge crocodile, Offalia becomes a Huge elephant, and Morgantha remains enveloped in shadow forms.

Each member assumes new combat strategies and abilities typical to their transformed bodies: Bella has Bite, Tail, and Tail Swipe attacks; Offalia uses Gore, Charge, Stomp, Tusks, Fiend's Trumpet, and Enervating Ring; Morgantha gains Eyebite, Inflict Nightmares, Matron's Rebuke, Shadow Step, and Absorb Elements reactions.
They retain all previous statistics but gain enhanced combat power in their new monstrous forms, making them more dangerous than before.
The form change occurs instantly upon reaching zero HP, transitioning directly into a phase of increased threat level.
foreshadowing: As a transformational stage, representing the coven's true fury and the potential for further escalation in their conflict with the party.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Strahd von Zarovich’s eldest wife and vampire spawn.
Morgantha reveals her existence as a hidden conspirator within Strahd’s household, adding to the layered deception in the overarching narrative.
Sasha is mentioned with delight by Morgantha, indicating that she finds this betrayal fascinating or even amusing.
The presence of Sasha hints at potential internal conflict within Strahd’s inner circle and suggests possible future plotlines where the party may encounter her directly or indirectly.
Her nature as a vampire spawn also ties into other vampiric-related lore throughout the setting.
foreshadowing: Sasha likely has a major part in later story arcs involving Strahd's family or his secret schemes, adding intrigue to future developments inside the castle.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A hidden temple of forbidden lore said to be guarded by Strahd von Zarovich where he first met the entity with whom he forged his pact.
Knowledge of the temple’s exact whereabouts is withheld by Morgantha, indicating that it is a crucial piece of information in a larger narrative puzzle.
Morgantha mentions having no knowledge of this secret yet knows enough about its nature to be meaningful to the players involved.
Serves as a possible future location for exploration and discovery.
It represents an artifact or relic significant to both Strahd’s personal history and the overall cosmic forces affecting the campaign.
foreshadowing: This temple is implied to be one of key landmarks connected to Strahd's pact and possibly central clues in locating other sacred elements of his domain.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Located at the edge of the Mists, marked by a circle of megaliths in constant fog.
Demarcates the boundary for an area associated with the Seeker of the Ladies Three shrine mentioned in Arc T - The Three Fanes.
Players can see the megaliths from Old Bonegrinder’s hilltop after approaching to the edge of the valley.
The area is heavily obscured by fog, and entering it results in a confrontation with four vampiric mists that attack the party.
Symbolizes a threshold for accessing other regions tied to divine powers or ancient shrines.
foreshadowing: The presence of the Mountain Fane suggests links between Old Bonegrinder, Strahd's domain, and wider mythos involving the three ladies and their shrines—especially since it involves a sacred site near the hilltop windmill.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Part of Arc T - The Three Fanes which is connected to the Mountain Fane near the windmill.
References a larger set of shrines tied to mysterious power forces in the Mists.
Involves a significant lore element related to divine worship or ancient knowledge that intersects with the coven's plans.
The three fanes likely form part of the key locations referenced in Strahd's escape narrative, possibly including the amber temple as well.
Represents sites of interest for players exploring beyond their initial conflict with Old Bonegrinder.
foreshadowing: Links to a broader campaign arc involving the three divine sources or guardians whose interaction could alter the nature of Strahd's dominion.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Former guard of Castle Ravenloft who gave orders to kill Strahd on Sergei's wedding day.
Was turned into a vampire spawn by Strahd, then sealed in a vault beneath Wachterhaus for 400 years.
Rises as a gallows speaker after his death, avenging the Wachter family and seeking justice.
The source of a curse that affected the Wachter lineage and caused his own death from starvation.
Holds the Tome of Strahd, which he stole from Wachterhaus after killing Lady Wachter's parents.
foreshadowing: His character arc provides emotional closure to Lady Wachter's history and revenge plot
Connects to themes in the Tome of Strahd and future storylines involving the Fanes of Barovia

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A powerful artifact containing the inner thoughts and secrets of Strahd von Zarovich.
Locked by an arcane lock spell at 5th level, requiring pass phrase 'I come in Dostron's memory' to open.
Contains historical accounts about Sergei's death, Tatyana's fate, and the creation of the land of Barovia.
Has diagrams and symbols hidden by a cipher, deciphered by the lich Exethanter.
Is kept within Wachterhaus after being stolen by Leo Dilisnya upon his mother’s murder.
foreshadowing: Its content will reveal more secrets about Strahd's history in the campaign
The cipher will be crucial information in later storylines, especially involving Exethanter

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: The location where the etherealness and restoration rituals are performed.
A chamber with a black pentagram on the floor, large wooden chairs at each point of the pentagram.
Has thousands of scratch marks and blood-stained carvings on walls including 'Traitor' and 'In Silence, Death'.
Carved by Leo Dilisnya using his vampiric claws, indicating his imprisonment beneath Wachterhaus.
Contains a three-foot-high carving of a dagger, representing the Ba’al Verzi guild.
foreshadowing: Connected to Leo Dilisnya's background through historical carvings and the leyline beneath it
Serves as both a setting for the restoration ritual and the climax in a battle with Leo's ghost

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A secret assassins' guild belonging to Leo Dilisnya.
Leo betrayed his order’s vows when attempting to kill Strahd, betraying its core values.
The motto of the guild 'In Silence, Death', appears etched in the walls of the cult headquarters.
Has dagger weapons that are central in Leo's avenger form during combat.
Connected to Leo Dilisnya's backstory and motives to avenge his family.
foreshadowing: References to this organization connect back to the deeper political intrigue of Barovia
Its dagger is key to the plot involving the sealing mechanisms of fane corruption

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: A natural magical source located beneath Wachterhaus which Victor identifies for the ritual.
Provides power needed to sustain the *etherealness* ritual.
Found by Victor using his magical knowledge and resources.
Used in placing players into the Ethereal Plane as part of the restoration process.
Connected to the cultural and supernatural history of Wachterhaus.
foreshadowing: Establishes magical significance of Wachterhaus beyond its immediate location
Foreshadows connections between various leyline sources in the greater setting

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc H - The Lost Soul.md
bullets: Giant spiders that pass through the Ethereal Plane, noted by Victor near ruins of Vallaki.
Characterized as creatures with a specific magical or ethereal nature.
Mentioned in *Ethereal Entities* book as referenced by Victor.
Present as an encounter threat outside of main narrative arcs.
Noted particularly because of their appearance in the southwest of Vallaki near ruins.
foreshadowing: Indicates increased supernatural activity during campaign
May appear again with later encounters requiring exploration

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Krezk is a suspicious village that won't allow outsiders in without a task.
The burgomaster of Krezk is Baron Dmitri Krezkov.
Baron Krezkov has a son named Ilya Krezkov who went missing.
Players can seek entry to Krezk by retrieving information about Ilya through Baba Lysaga's scrying spell.
Once entry is gained, players may access the Abbey of Saint Markovia for a cure for cackle fever.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Baron Krezkov is the burgomaster of Krezk and father of Ilya and Kala.
He has a wife named Anna Krezkova and a brother named Zuleika Toranescu.
Dmitri is secretly a werewolf and deeply anxious about his family's curse.
He asks players to find his missing son, Ilya, through Baba Lysaga's scrying.
Baron Krezkov is known for being grim yet warm in public, but privately grieving and guilt-ridden.
foreshadowing: Baron Krezkov has hidden knowledge about family curse and a connection to the Abbot.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Ilya is Baron Dmitri Krezkov's elder son and the missing person sought in scrying spell.
He disappeared on the night of Octyavr 22, near a full moon.
Baron Krezkov suspects his family curse is affecting Ilya.
According to Baba Lysaga’s scrying, Ilya was captured and chained by a werewolf.
His whereabouts are tied into secrets regarding Mother Night’s cult.
foreshadowing: Ilya's abduction is connected to Mother Night cult, which will be relevant to the campaign.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Baba Lysaga is a powerful, mad and fickle witch residing in the ruins of Berez.
She resides in a hut perched atop a massive stump.
Players must convince her to perform a scrying spell using a hair of Ilya and a gold bracelet.
If angered, she may polymorph players into goats and lock them in her goat pen.
Baba Lysaga can see the location and circumstances of Ilya’s captivity through *scrying* spell.
foreshadowing: Baba Lysaga is connected to Strahd through Ludmilla, and her cult practices involve lycanthropy.
She has ties to Mother Night and holds dark knowledge relevant to players' curse.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Wensencia is a Barovian witch studying under Baba Lysaga for three months.
She believes in Strahd’s return as a dark messiah and follows his bride, Ludmilla Vilisevic.
She greets players upon their arrival at Berez and guides them to Baba Lysaga.
Wensencia serves Baba Lysaga and is hostile toward anyone who threatens or disrespects her master.
She can provide insight into how one approaches Baba Lysaga and what she might expect.
foreshadowing: Wensencia plays a key role in the cultic practices connected to Strahd’s revival.
She may appear again during other events involving Mother Night or Strahd’s influence.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: A greater strix is a large scarecrow-like creature made of burlap, wood and skins.
It warns Baba Lysaga of intruders approaching Berez by flying south through mist.
It does not engage in battle but flies to alert the witch.
The strix appears at the Luna River Crossroads just before players reach Berez.
Baba Lysaga created this artifact for capturing ravens, whom she loathes.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Phase spiders are summoned within the web circle of Berez upon players entering.
They use ethereal jaunt ability and briefly flicker between planes.
Five phase spiders attack if players enter the center of the web circle.
They serve as guardians of Baba Lysaga's sacred space in the swamp ruins.
Player encounters with these spiders should be used for atmospheric danger, prior to final goal.
foreshadowing: Phase Spiders may appear in other fane locations within Barovia.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
surface_forms: Abbot of the Abbey of Saint Markovia
canonical_hint: Abbot of the Abbey of Saint Markovia
bullets: The Abbot is an angel named Ithuriel, sent to honor Saint Markovia.
He is the creator of a flesh golem Vasilka and the resurrector of Kala Krezkova.
He possesses healing touch that cures lycanthropy without fee.
The Abbot operates from the Abbey of Saint Markovia and is connected to the Morninglord.
He may not be aware of all truths about his history or the corruption within the Abbey.
foreshadowing: The Abbot may be revealed as a greater figure in Strahd's conflict with the Morninglord.
Players might uncover more about the Abbey’s hidden history and its connection to lycanthropy.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Built atop Mount Baratok by a cleric named Saint Markovia, who was killed by Strahd.
After decades of madness and cannibalism, it was reopened by the current Abbot.
The Abbey houses a blessed pool where wolfsbane grows and is used to cure lycanthropy.
It is connected with the Morninglord and serves as a place of spiritual danger and conflict.
Visiting the Abbey will eventually connect players with broader campaign plots involving Strahd.
foreshadowing: The Abbey's lore and the Abbot's true nature are central to future story arcs like Arc K - The Fallen Abbey.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Mother Night is the patron deity associated with lycanthropy and magic rituals.
She is a divine figure for the "Ladies Three"—a cult or divine entity of darkness.
Baba Lysaga sees her influence on Ilya, who was captured by a werewolf serving Mother Night.
Players will encounter more references to Mother Night throughout their journey through Barovia.
Cult followers of Mother Night are linked to Strahd's influence and supernatural activities.
foreshadowing: Mother Night cult is tied to the broader supernatural forces affecting players' curse.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Located within Berez ruins near the swamp, marking the site of a former monument to Marina.
The monument is associated with King Dostron and the damming of Luna River tributary.
It lies in the area marked by the Swamp Fane and was part of Strahd's punishment for her death.
Players might explore this location if they travel around ruins beyond the main path.
References suggest that Marina’s presence was historically tied to changes in land and magical events.
foreshadowing: Marina's role connects to larger themes of past history, betrayal, and Strahd's control over Barovia.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Zuleika is the brother of Baron Dmitri Krezkov.
She is a werewolf cleric connected to Mother Night cult.
Her role is connected with family curse and broader lycanthropic events in Krezk.
Players will learn more about her involvement during future arcs related to the werewolf curse.
This hints at familial ties between certain members of Barovia's nobility and supernatural factions.
foreshadowing: More details on Zuleika’s activities may emerge through connections with lycanthropy, curses, or Strahd.

source_file: Act II - The Shadowed Town/Arc I - The Walls of Krezk.md
bullets: Anna is the wife of Baron Dmitri Krezkov.
She is part of the Krezk family facing a mysterious curse and disappearances.
Players may be informed indirectly about her role or fate in future narrative.
Her presence reflects the emotional toll of the family crisis on both children and parents.
Family dynamics and secret histories are linked to her character arc.
foreshadowing: A deeper backstory for Anna could reveal her role in the curse or her connection to lycanthropy.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A member of the Martikov family who approaches the players for help investigating a wine shipment shortage at the Wizard of Wines.
Is the father of Davian Martikov, though he avoids discussing their relationship.
Has sent ravens to investigate but none have returned.
Mentions that Baba Lysaga's strix have been patrolling the valley in greater numbers.
Son of Davian Martikov and brother to Adrian, Elvir, and Elric.
Accused by his father of stealing the gemstone from the winery, leaving with Danika Dorakova.
His claim of seeing a raven steal the gem led to a rift with his father.
Sent a message for help after being driven out of the winery by druids.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: The patriarch of the Martikov family and owner of the Wizard of Wines.
Lives in a hidden cavern beneath the winery with his family due to an invasion by druids known as the Forest Folk.
Asks the players to defeat the Forest Folk and break the staff controlling the blights to reclaim the winery.
Instructs the players to retrieve the stolen enchanted gemstone that was used in grape growing.
Patriarch of the Martikov family and leader of the Keepers of the Feather.
The father of Urwin, Adrian, Elvir, and Elric Martikov.
Blamed his son Urwin for the theft of a gemstone from the winery ten years prior.
Banished his son Elric for defying orders to not fight in Castle Ravenloft.
Has a long history with the druids of Yester Hill and their enmity with the winery.
Leader of the Keepers of the Feather and a wereraven.
His grandfather was Livius Martikov—a historical figure involved in stealing relics.
Founded his group after years of resistance against Strahd through espionage.
Provides magic items including the Keeper's Whistle to help players.
He overheard a prophetic Tarokka reading involving Strahd and the artifacts.
foreshadowing: Davian serves as a guide and helper for players, but his own identity as wereraven could be important later.
The prophecy involving Strahd may foreshadow upcoming threats or challenges.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A former employee of the Wizard of Wines who accompanies players on their journey.
Is a secret wereraven and member of the Keepers of the Feather.
Has a blue streak in her hair, which is a hint to her true nature if questioned directly.
Claims she is not a druid, and that she detests trees; she's seen a wolf with a bad face but not Strazni.
Member of the Keepers of the Feather and recognizes Davian from a cloak.
Betrothed to Elric Martikov but now mourns his loss.
Seems to have been present during Elric’s fall, possibly with a connection to him.
A wereraven and member of the Keepers of the Feather.
Has a personal contact named Kavan in the Svalich Wood.
Is betrothed to Elric Martikov, who she believes died.
Uses a dagger from her boot that can be used to make a cut on her palm during summoning rituals.
Appears to have been eavesdropping on Strahd at Tser Pool.
foreshadowing: Her role as a Keepers of the Feather agent will be revealed later in campaign progression.
Her true identity as wereraven might have implications for future combat or lore revelations.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: Rejoins the party after previously being in the village of Vallaki.
Decides to leave Vallaki and accompany the players in their fight against Strahd.
Shares motivation for not wanting to hide any longer, after her experiences with Izek Strazni and seeing the importance of fighting directly.
Provides healing capabilities through training as a healer, and is skilled in swordplay.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: Lord of Castle Ravenloft, who appears on the path and warns players about threats from Yester Hill.
Has ordered that Ireena be left unharmed during her travels.
Offers a wager to the party if they agree to oppose the druids and fight Wintersplinter.
The master of Castle Ravenloft and one of the primary antagonists.
Rides a nightmare named Beucephalus.
Has several vampiric brides including Ludmilla Vilisevic.
Encounters players near Kavan's cairn and at Yester Hill, and offers them a wager related to stopping the Forest Folk ritual.
Has prior knowledge of Muriel's wereraven nature.
foreshadowing: The Whispering Wall and its visions will be explored later in the campaign.
Interaction with Strahd introduces potential rewards related to the player's journey inside the wall.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A group of druids who have invaded the Wizard of Wines winery.
They are led by Svarog and are known to worship Strahd as a deity.
They nurture a cutting of the Gulthias tree within the winery.
Plan to summon a being called Wintersplinter at Yester Hill.
A group of druids from Yester Hill who are enemies of the Keepers and Martikovs.
Aligned with Baba Lysaga and involved in various attacks on the winery.
Seem to be preparing an evil ritual involving a tree blight called Wintersplinter.
foreshadowing: The druids’ lair is located on Yester Hill, a key location for upcoming encounters.
Their leader Svarog will be met and challenged during the climax of the arc.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A secret society dedicated to countering Strahd's influence in Barovia.
Muriel is a member and agent under their command and direct contact with URwin Martikov.
Their activities involve reconnaissance, spreading prophetic information, and sabotaging enemies of Barovia’s freedom.
An ancient secret society dedicated to opposing the forces of darkness in Barovia.
Uses ravens as messengers and spies, operating under a non-combat approach.
Founded by Livius Martikov during his adventures with Ismark Antonovich and Lugdana.
Seems to involve many barovians including Muriel Vinshaw.
A secret society of wereravens working against Strahd.
Operates through intelligence gathering rather than direct confrontation.
Founded by Livius Martikov, Davian's grandfather.
Has historical connections to the theft of *Holy Symbol of Ravenkind* and *Tome of Strahd*.
Uses agents like Muriel for field operations.
foreshadowing: The Keepers play an important role in guiding the players through Barovia's political layers.
The group may be involved in future intrigue or missions involving supernatural threats.
Use of the Keeper's Whistle ties into their operational methods.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A winery located to the southwest end of Barovian valley.
Was invaded by members of the Forest Folk; now inhabited by Martikov family in underground caverns.
Stores grapes for wines like Purple Grapemash No. 3 and Red Dragon Crush.
Has an enchanted gemstone, stolen by the druids, used for grape growing.
An old winery located to the west in Barovia.
Constructed on ancient lands, originally a place for the production of wine from enchanted gemstones.
Has been under attack from druids and their allies after thefts of gems from it.
foreshadowing: This site is central to the plot progression in this arc—players investigate here before traveling to Yester Hill for Wintersplinter.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: Leader of the Forest Folk druids attacking the Wizard of Wines winery.
Wields a yew wand which is sought after as part of an objective for Kavan after defeating him.
A druid naturalist who wields a wand made from petrified wood.
Is the one who retrieves Svarog's wand, as mentioned by Kavan.
Has special abilities such as 'Gust of Wind' and 'Bind Elements'.
Initially part of the circle, but becomes an active combatant when players enter the circle.
foreshadowing: Player characters will fight Svarog in the climactic battle on Yester Hill.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A terraced mound in the Svalich Woods where the Forest Folk have set up their ritual to summon Wintersplinter.
Home base for the druids and also sanctuary of Gulthias tree that grows atop it.
The site of a planned summoning involving the Tree Blight.
A hill in Barovia near which the druids reside.
Has a Gulthias Tree atop it, whose cuttings were planted in the winery.
Home to the druids and likely where their evil ritual is currently being prepared.
A hilltop where the druids conduct their ritual.
Contains a stone circle formed by black boulders.
Has a Gulthias Tree at its peak, which is central to the ritual.
Home to the Forest Folk druids and their activities.
foreshadowing: Yester Hill is central location for climactic confrontation between players and Forest Folk druids.
This is where the gemstone must be retrieved from Wintersplinter’s heart.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A being that the Forest Folk attempt to summon using a ritual at Yester Hill.
Is described as a fearsome tree blight threatening the Wizard of Wines winery.
Can be defeated by players and its heart contains the stolen enchanted gemstone.
A feared figure or entity connected to Yester Hill.
Expected to march upon the winery soon, threatening its fate.
Linked with druids’ plans and may be related to the tree blight.
The result of a druidic ritual to create new life from the Gulthias Tree.
Born under specific conditions in the Yester Hill ritual involving an enchanted gemstone.
The final goal of the ritual; destruction of this entity is a key objective for players.
foreshadowing: Wintersplinter will be the primary antagonist in the final section of this arc.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: Strahd's nightmare, ridden by him during an encounter on the path to Yester Hill.
Serves as a steed for Strahd and represents his formidable presence.
Strahd’s nightmare steed, depicted in the Crypt 39 (p. 93).
Has 104 hit points and an 'Ethereal Escape' feature that allows it to use 'ethereal stride'.
Carries Strahd's animated armor in its saddlebags.
Appears both at Kavan's Cairn and Yester Hill.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: An ancient chieftain of the Forest Folk who appears as a spirit to provide guidance.
Warns players about Svarog’s strength; invites them back to his cairn if they defeat him.
Shares history regarding schism between Forest and Mountain Folk.
A ghost of a former chieftain from the Balinok Mountains, known as 'Blood-Drinker'.
Once stole the Huntress's Spear and defaced shrines of the Ladies Three.
Was banished by the druids after his actions, and cursed to dwell in a lonely cairn.
Provides information about the druids of Yester Hill and their plans.
Has a cairn marked with runes describing him as 'Forsaken of the Rozana' and 'First and Last Chieftain of the Balinok Mountains'.
Former chieftain among the First Folk, who arrived in the valley long before his own birth.
Wielded Svarog's wand, once used by those who connected his people to the Rozana.
Defied his people's strictures and ventured into the Whispering Wall where he learned secrets of the Huntress’s sacred spear.
Warred on other tribes and re-united them beneath his banner after stealing the spear.
Banished from Soldav by the druids for hubris and betrayal, buried in a lonely cairn far from his forefathers.
foreshadowing: Kavan’s lore introduces the Mountain Folk as a faction in need of exploration.
Reveals the existence of Soldav in Tsolenka Pass as a potential location if players seek to reverse land corruption.
His tale ties into the conflict between the Forest Folk and Mountain Folk.
His burial site may be significant for future rituals or quests.
The location of Svarog's wand may be key to further developments with the First Folk.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
source_anchor: J2b. The Path to Tsolenka Pass
bullets: A tribe of hunter-gatherers native to the Balinok Mountains.
Live separately from Forest Folk and still hold old beliefs, worshipping ancient goddesses of Barovia.
Are not servants of Strahd and are currently active due to their search for something unknown.
Have a hidden settlement called Soldav in Tsolenka Pass.
Two factions derived from the First Folk following a schism during Strahd's invasion.
Those opposed to Strahd retreated to Soldav (Mountain Folk), while others stayed in Svalich Wood (Forest Folk).
The split originated from historical conflicts with Kavan and King Dostron.
They are not aligned with each other and view one another suspiciously.
foreshadowing: Their hidden settlement is mentioned as a key destination if players wish to find ways to reverse land corruption.
A meeting with the Mountain Folk is required to restore the spear stolen by Kavan.
Conflict within these groups may be exploited for dramatic effect or factional alliances.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A vampiric tree growing atop Yester Hill.
Nurtured by the Forest Folk within the winery, and gives birth to blights.
Connected to the overall theme of corruption in Barovia.
A cursed tree growing atop Yester Hill.
Used in the winery to create blights and control environments.
A dark, corrupted tree atop Yester Hill.
Plays an active role in the ritual being performed by Forest Folk druids.
Is an accomplice to the druids' will and grows stronger with cursed blood.
Has a grove that contains the Forest Fane shrine.
foreshadowing: Gulthias is tied to the blight creatures that spawn from it, and thus influences the battle at Yester Hill.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: Twisted plant creatures created by the Gulthias tree.
Inhabit the Wizard of Wines and act as servants to the Forest Folk druids.
Have a deceptive presence in forms such as vine clumps.
foreshadowing: These blights will become part of combat encounters in this arc, especially near Yester Hill.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A creature threatening to lay siege to the Wizard of Wines winery.
Is a form of Wintersplinter and is fought in the climax at Yester Hill.
A magical tree creature grown from cuttings.
Planted at the winery as part of the druids’ plan to control access.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
bullets: A mysterious structure located west of Yester Hill where Strahd offers a reward for those who defeat him.
Contains misted depths with visions of deepest desires.
A mysterious wall near Yester Hill.
Located beside which the druids of the Forest Folk dwell.

source_file: Act III - The Broken Land/Arc J - The Stolen Gem.md
wikilink_seeds: [[Arc J - The Stolen Gem#The Shroud of Mist|The Shroud of Mist]]
bullets: A foggy area mentioned as a possible path related to the Whispering Wall.
Connected with illusions and dreams in a hidden lore aspect involving Strahd.
A mysterious place or area that relates to the overall plot.
Connected with events involving the magical environment and concealment.
A spell effect used by Ludmilla to block the players.
Has a 60-foot radius and lasts for 10 minutes or until dismissed.
Causes creatures who come into contact with it for the first time on a turn to take necrotic damage and be slowed.
Used as a barrier during the sequence of combat involving Ludmilla.
